label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is whether following these steps will bring me back to the starting point. Let's break down each instruction step by step. First, the instructions say to "always face forward." That means my direction doesn't change throughout these steps. So regardless of which way I turn, I keep facing the same direction. Wait, actually, the problem says "always face forward," which probably means I don't turn or change my direction. So my orientation remains fixed. So if I start facing north, I stay facing north throughout all the steps. Now, the steps are: 1. Take 10 steps backward. 2. Take 7 steps backward. 3. Take 8 steps right. 4. Take 6 steps right. 5. Take 3 steps left. Let me visualize this. Let's assume I start at point (0,0) facing north. But since direction might not be necessary if we just track movement. Let me think in terms of coordinates. If I face forward, moving backward would be in the opposite direction of my facing. Moving to the right or left would be perpendicular to my facing direction. Since I'm facing forward (let's say north), moving right would be east, and left would be west. So, starting at (0,0), facing north. First, take 10 steps backward. Since I'm facing north, moving backward is south. So moving 10 steps south from (0,0) would bring me to (0, -10). Second, take 7 steps backward. Again, south. So from (0, -10), moving 7 steps south is (0, -17). Third, take 8 steps right. Facing north, right is east. So moving 8 steps east from (0, -17) is (8, -17). Fourth, take 6 steps right. Again, east. So from (8, -17), moving 6 steps east is (14, -17). Fifth, take 3 steps left. Facing north, left is west. So 3 steps west from (14, -17) is (11, -17). So the final position is (11, -17). The starting point was (0,0). Clearly, I haven't returned to the starting point. So the answer should be "No." Wait, let me double-check my direction. When moving right or left, if I'm facing north, right is east and left is west. So when I take 8 steps right, that's east, adding to the x-coordinate. Then 6 more steps right (east) adds more to x. Then 3 steps left (west) subtracts from x. So total movement in x: 8 + 6
